server.name: database-server
server.description: Comprehensive database server supporting PostgreSQL, MySQL, and SQLite with natural language SQL query capabilities. Enables AI agents to interact with databases through both direct SQL and natural language queries.
server.title: MCP Database Server

tools.name: query_database
tools.description: Execute natural language queries against the database and convert them to SQL

arguments.name: query
arguments.desc: Natural language description of what you want to query from the database

tools.name: list_tables
tools.description: List all available tables in the current database

tools.name: describe_table
tools.description: Get detailed schema information for a specific table including columns, types, and constraints

arguments.name: table_name
arguments.desc: Name of the table to describe

tools.name: execute_sql
tools.description: Execute raw SQL queries with safety checks and validation

arguments.name: query
arguments.desc: SQL query to execute

tools.name: connect_to_database
tools.description: Connect to a new database using provided connection details

arguments.name: connection_string
arguments.desc: Database connection string (e.g., postgresql://user:pass@host:port/db)

tools.name: get_connection_examples
tools.description: Get example connection strings for different database types (SQLite, PostgreSQL, MySQL)

tools.name: get_current_database_info
tools.description: Get information about the currently connected database including type, version, and connection status

config.description: Configure the database connection. Supports SQLite, PostgreSQL, and MySQL databases with comprehensive querying capabilities.
config.name: database-server

database_url.description: Connection string for your database. Examples: SQLite: sqlite+aiosqlite:///data/mydb.db, PostgreSQL: postgresql+asyncpg://user:password@localhost:5432/mydb, MySQL: mysql+aiomysql://user:password@localhost:3306/mydb

tools.name: client_list
tools.description: Get a list of connected clients to the Redis server.

tools.name: create_vector_index_hash
tools.description:
Create a Redis 8 vector similarity index using HNSW on a Redis hash.

This function sets up a Redis index for approximate nearest neighbor (ANN)
search using the HNSW algorithm and float32 vector embeddings.

arguments.name: dim
arguments.desc: The dimensionality of the vectors stored under the vector_field.

arguments.name: distance_metric
arguments.desc: The distance function to use (e.g., 'COSINE', 'L2', 'IP').

arguments.name: index_name
arguments.desc: The name of the Redis index to create. Unless specifically required, use the default name for the index.

arguments.name: prefix
arguments.desc: The key prefix used to identify documents to index (e.g., 'doc:'). Unless specifically required, use the default prefix.

arguments.name: vector_field
arguments.desc: The name of the vector field to be indexed for similarity search. Unless specifically required, use the default field name

tools.name: dbsize
tools.description: Get the number of keys stored in the Redis database

tools.name: delete
tools.description: Delete a Redis key.

tools.name: expire
tools.description: Set an expiration time for a Redis key.

arguments.name: expire_seconds
arguments.desc: Time in seconds after which the key should expire.

arguments.name: name
arguments.desc: The Redis key.

tools.name: get
tools.description: Get a Redis string value.

tools.name: get_index_info
tools.description: Retrieve schema and information about a specific Redis index using FT.INFO.

tools.name: get_indexed_keys_number
tools.description: Retrieve the number of indexed keys by the index

tools.name: get_indexes
tools.description:
List of indexes in the Redis database

Returns:
    str: A JSON string containing the list of indexes or an error message.

tools.name: get_vector_from_hash
tools.description: Retrieve a vector from a Redis hash and convert it back from binary blob.

arguments.name: name
arguments.desc: The Redis hash key.

arguments.name: vector_field
arguments.desc: The field name inside the hash. Unless specifically required, use the default field name

tools.name: hdel
tools.description: Delete a field from a Redis hash.

arguments.name: key
arguments.desc: The field name inside the hash.

arguments.name: name
arguments.desc: The Redis hash key.

tools.name: hexists
tools.description: Check if a field exists in a Redis hash.

arguments.name: key
arguments.desc: The field name inside the hash.

arguments.name: name
arguments.desc: The Redis hash key.

tools.name: hget
tools.description: Get the value of a field in a Redis hash.

arguments.name: key
arguments.desc: The field name inside the hash.

arguments.name: name
arguments.desc: The Redis hash key.

tools.name: hgetall
tools.description: Get all fields and values from a Redis hash.

arguments.name: name
arguments.desc: The Redis hash key.

tools.name: hset
tools.description: Set a field in a hash stored at key with an optional expiration time.

arguments.name: key
arguments.desc: The field name inside the hash.

arguments.name: name
arguments.desc: The Redis hash key.

arguments.name: value
arguments.desc: The value to set.

arguments.name: expire_seconds
arguments.desc: Optional; time in seconds after which the key should expire.

tools.name: hybrid_search
tools.description:
Perform a hybrid search combining a Redis filter expression with KNN vector similarity.

Hybrid search pre-filters documents by metadata before ranking by vector similarity —
the standard pattern for production RAG and semantic search pipelines.

Filter expression examples:
    "*"                              → no filter, pure vector search (same as vector_search_hash)
    "@category:{news}"              → tag filter
    "@year:[2020 2024]"             → numeric range
    "@lang:{en} @year:[2022 +inf]"  → combined tag + range
    "@title:redis"                  → full-text match on a text field

Full filter syntax: https://redis.io/docs/latest/develop/interact/search-and-query/query/

arguments.name: query_vector
arguments.desc: List of floats to use as the query vector.

arguments.name: filter_expression
arguments.desc: Redis filter expression to restrict candidates before KNN ranking.

arguments.name: index_name
arguments.desc: Name of the Redis index (default: 'vector_index').

arguments.name: k
arguments.desc: Number of nearest neighbors to return.

arguments.name: return_fields
arguments.desc: Additional fields to include in results (optional).

arguments.name: vector_field
arguments.desc: Name of the indexed vector field (default: 'vector').

tools.name: info
tools.description: Get Redis server information and statistics.

arguments.name: section
arguments.desc: The section of the info command (default, memory, cpu, etc.).

tools.name: json_del
tools.description: Delete a JSON value from Redis at a given path.

arguments.name: name
arguments.desc: The Redis key where the JSON document is stored.

arguments.name: path
arguments.desc: The JSON path to delete (default: root '$').

tools.name: json_get
tools.description: Retrieve a JSON value from Redis at a given path.

arguments.name: name
arguments.desc: The Redis key where the JSON document is stored.

arguments.name: path
arguments.desc: The JSON path to retrieve (default: root '$').

tools.name: json_set
tools.description: Set a JSON value in Redis at a given path with an optional expiration time.

arguments.name: name
arguments.desc: The Redis key where the JSON document is stored.

arguments.name: path
arguments.desc: The JSON path where the value should be set.

arguments.name: value
arguments.desc: The JSON value to store (as JSON string, or will be auto-converted).

arguments.name: expire_seconds
arguments.desc: Optional; time in seconds after which the key should expire.

tools.name: llen
tools.description: Get the length of a Redis list.

tools.name: lpop
tools.description: Remove and return the first element from a Redis list.

tools.name: lpush
tools.description: Push a value onto the left of a Redis list and optionally set an expiration time.

tools.name: lrange
tools.description:
Get elements from a Redis list within a specific range.

Returns:
str: A JSON string containing the list of elements or an error message.

tools.name: lrem
tools.description: Remove elements from a Redis list.

arguments.name: count
arguments.desc: Number of elements to remove (0 = all, positive = from head, negative = from tail)

arguments.name: element
arguments.desc: The element value to remove

arguments.name: name
arguments.desc: The name of the list

tools.name: publish
tools.description: Publish a message to a Redis channel.

arguments.name: channel
arguments.desc: The Redis channel to publish to.

arguments.name: message
arguments.desc: The message to send.

tools.name: rename
tools.description: Renames a Redis key from old_key to new_key.

tools.name: rpop
tools.description: Remove and return the last element from a Redis list.

tools.name: rpush
tools.description: Push a value onto the right of a Redis list and optionally set an expiration time.

tools.name: sadd
tools.description: Add a value to a Redis set with an optional expiration time.

arguments.name: name
arguments.desc: The Redis set key.

arguments.name: value
arguments.desc: The value to add to the set.

arguments.name: expire_seconds
arguments.desc: Optional; time in seconds after which the set should expire.

tools.name: scan_all_keys
tools.description:
Scan and return ALL keys matching a pattern using multiple SCAN iterations.

This function automatically handles the SCAN cursor iteration to collect all matching keys.
It's safer than KEYS * for large databases but will still collect all results in memory.

⚠️  WARNING: With very large datasets (millions of keys), this may consume significant memory.
For large-scale operations, consider using scan_keys() with manual iteration instead.

arguments.name: batch_size
arguments.desc: Number of keys to scan per iteration (default 100).

arguments.name: pattern
arguments.desc: Pattern to match keys against (default is "*" for all keys).

tools.name: scan_keys
tools.description:
Scan keys in the Redis database using the SCAN command (non-blocking, production-safe).

⚠️  IMPORTANT: This returns PARTIAL results from one iteration. Use scan_all_keys()
to get ALL matching keys, or call this function multiple times with the returned cursor
until cursor becomes 0.

The SCAN command iterates through the keyspace in small chunks, making it safe to use
on large databases without blocking other operations.

arguments.name: count
arguments.desc: Hint for the number of keys to return per iteration (default 100).

arguments.name: cursor
arguments.desc: The cursor position to start scanning from (0 to start from beginning).

arguments.name: pattern
arguments.desc: Pattern to match keys against (default is "*" for all keys).

tools.name: search_redis_documents
tools.description:
Search Redis documentation and knowledge base to learn about Redis concepts and use cases.

This tool exposes updated and curated documentation, and must be invoked every time the user wants to learn more in areas including:

**Common Use Cases:**
- Session Management: User session storage and management
- Caching: Application-level and database query caching
- Rate Limiting: API throttling and request limiting
- Leaderboards: Gaming and ranking systems
- Semantic Search: AI-powered similarity search
- Agentic Workflows: AI agent state and memory management
- RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation): Vector storage for AI applications
- Real-time Analytics: Counters, metrics, and time-series data
- Message Queues: Task queues and job processing
- Geospatial: Location-based queries and proximity search

arguments.name: question
arguments.desc: The question about Redis concepts, data structures, features, or use cases

tools.name: set
tools.description: Set a Redis string value with an optional expiration time.

tools.name: set_vector_in_hash
tools.description: Store a vector as a field in a Redis hash.

arguments.name: name
arguments.desc: The Redis hash key.

arguments.name: vector
arguments.desc: The vector (list of numbers) to store in the hash.

arguments.name: vector_field
arguments.desc: The field name inside the hash. Unless specifically required, use the default field name

tools.name: smembers
tools.description: Get all members of a Redis set.

arguments.name: name
arguments.desc: The Redis set key.

tools.name: srem
tools.description: Remove a value from a Redis set.

arguments.name: name
arguments.desc: The Redis set key.

arguments.name: value
arguments.desc: The value to remove from the set.

tools.name: subscribe
tools.description: Subscribe to a Redis channel.

arguments.name: channel
arguments.desc: The Redis channel to subscribe to.

tools.name: type
tools.description: Returns the string representation of the type of the value stored at key

tools.name: unsubscribe
tools.description: Unsubscribe from a Redis channel.

arguments.name: channel
arguments.desc: The Redis channel to unsubscribe from.

tools.name: vector_search_hash
tools.description: Perform a KNN vector similarity search using Redis 8 or later version on vectors stored in hash data structures.

arguments.name: query_vector
arguments.desc: List of floats to use as the query vector.

arguments.name: index_name
arguments.desc: Name of the Redis index. Unless specifically specified, use the default index name.

arguments.name: k
arguments.desc: Number of nearest neighbors to return.

arguments.name: return_fields
arguments.desc: List of fields to return (optional).

arguments.name: vector_field
arguments.desc: Name of the indexed vector field. Unless specifically required, use the default field name

tools.name: xack
tools.description: Acknowledge entries that were processed by a consumer group.

tools.name: xadd
tools.description: Add an entry to a Redis stream with an optional expiration time.

tools.name: xdel
tools.description: Delete an entry from a Redis stream.

tools.name: xgroup_create
tools.description: Create a consumer group for a Redis stream.

tools.name: xgroup_destroy
tools.description: Destroy a consumer group for a Redis stream.

tools.name: xrange
tools.description: Read entries from a Redis stream.

tools.name: xreadgroup
tools.description: Read entries from a Redis stream using a consumer group.

tools.name: zadd
tools.description: Add a member to a Redis sorted set with an optional expiration time.

tools.name: zrange
tools.description: Retrieve a range of members from a Redis sorted set.

tools.name: zrem
tools.description: Remove a member from a Redis sorted set.
